name: Allen v. Milligan
summary: Upheld the Gingles framework for Section 2 vote dilution claims and rejected Alabama's attempts to revise the test, affirming that race-conscious redistricting can remedy Section 2 violations.
significance: Central to the case as a nearly identical Section 2 challenge decided just two years prior. Robinson appellants argued it controls this case and forecloses revisiting Gingles; opponents argued their specific arguments were not raised or rejected there.
